title: Feature management: IFeatureMetadata implementation and a feature-gated code path
instruction: In the sandbox model, ship an optional behaviour behind a feature. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="feature-management"). Create: (1) a class DemoFastPostingFeature implementing IFeatureMetadata with the singleton `instance()` pattern (private static construct + static instance()), returning labelled name/summary/learn-more values and the correct enabled-by-default and can-disable answers; (2) a class DemoPostingService whose `public static str describe()` branches on `FeatureStateProvider::isFeatureEnabled(DemoFastPostingFeature::instance())` and returns a different labelled string per branch. Confirm the IFeatureMetadata member list with get_object_info/get_method before implementing it - an incomplete interface implementation fails the build. A boolean flag on a parameters table, or a macro flight, FAILS this case: the point is the feature-management contract. Both classes must build with zero BP errors.
